description: Add 1:1 and group call (up to 8 participants) support to QuantChat by reusing the LiveKit gateway with a 'chat-call' room type. Also implement QuantMax random video chat with a new matchmaking service that pairs users into ephemeral LiveKit rooms auto-destroyed on disconnect. Add TURN server Helm chart and E2E encrypted media option.
steps: Create apps/quantchat/backend/services/call.service.ts - CallService class that uses LiveKitGateway (imported pattern from quantmeet or instantiated locally) to: initiate1v1Call(callerId, calleeId), initiateGroupCall(initiatorId, participantIds[]), endCall(callId), generateCallToken(callId, userId). Room naming: chat-call:{callId}. Max 8 participants for group calls.

Create apps/quantchat/backend/routes/calls.ts - REST endpoints: POST /calls/initiate (1:1), POST /calls/group (group), POST /calls/:id/join, POST /calls/:id/leave, GET /calls/:id/token. Register in app.ts.
Update apps/quantchat/backend/app.ts to register the calls route.
Create services/matchmaking/ directory as a new workspace service with package.json, tsconfig.json, src/main.ts

Create services/matchmaking/src/matchmaker.ts - MatchmakingService class: maintains a Redis-backed waiting queue. joinQueue(userId, preferences) adds user. When two compatible users are queued, pairs them, creates an ephemeral LiveKit room (room name: max-random:{pairId}), returns tokens to both. On disconnect webhook, auto-destroys the room. Includes timeout (30s) to auto-remove from queue.
Create services/matchmaking/src/main.ts - Fastify app with health endpoint and WebSocket endpoint for queue management. Endpoints: POST /queue/join, POST /queue/leave, GET /queue/status.
Create services/matchmaking/package.json with dependencies: livekit-server-sdk, fastify, ioredis, @quant/server-core, @quant/common, @quant/health-server.
Create services/matchmaking/tsconfig.json following the pattern from services/ws-gateway/tsconfig.json.
Update apps/quantmax/backend/app.ts to add a /random-chat route that proxies to the matchmaking service or calls it directly.
Create apps/quantmax/backend/routes/random-chat.ts - endpoints for initiating random chat: POST /random-chat/join, POST /random-chat/leave, GET /random-chat/status.
Create infra/helm/turn/ directory with Chart.yaml, values.yaml, and templates/ for coturn deployment. Include credential rotation config (daily rotation via K8s CronJob that updates a Secret).

Add E2E encryption support: create packages/shared-ui/src/components/Media/E2EEncryption.tsx - component/hook that enables LiveKit's built-in E2EE (Insertable Streams / SFrame). Toggleable per-room as 'secure room' mode. Key exchange via a shared passphrase.

Write tests: apps/quantchat/backend/__tests__/call.service.test.ts (mock LiveKit SDK, verify room creation, token generation, participant limits), services/matchmaking/src/__tests__/matchmaker.test.ts (mock Redis, verify queue join/leave, pairing logic, timeout).
Run pnpm install, pnpm typecheck, pnpm test to verify everything passes.
acceptance_criteria: apps/quantchat/backend/services/call.service.ts exists with 1:1 and group call support (max 8)
apps/quantchat/backend/routes/calls.ts provides REST API for call management
services/matchmaking/ exists as a new workspace service with queue-based pairing
services/matchmaking creates ephemeral LiveKit rooms for paired users
infra/helm/turn/ contains a Helm chart for coturn with daily credential rotation
E2EEncryption component in shared-ui enables SFrame encryption for secure rooms
All unit tests pass for quantchat, quantmax, and matchmaking service
pnpm typecheck passes across the entire monorepo
